title: Search shows wrong results — finding archived tasks instead of active ones
body: Something is wrong with search. When I search for tasks, I'm getting results that include old archived tasks that should be hidden, and I'm not seeing some active tasks that I know exist. For example, I searched for 'Q2 planning' and got an archived task from last year but not the active one I created yesterday. This is happening for all my searches. My teammate says she sees the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — that's definitely not the expected behavior. To help us reproduce this, could you walk us through the exact steps? Specifically:

- Where are you performing the search? (e.g., the main search bar, a filtered view, the task list page)
- What exactly do you type or select when searching?
- After searching for 'Q2 planning', do you see the archived task mixed in with active results, or is it the *only* result returned?

A step-by-step walkthrough like "1. Go to X page, 2. Type Y in the search bar, 3. Click Z" would be really helpful for our developers to pinpoint the issue.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Sure, happy to walk through it. I use the main search bar at the top of the task list page. Here's what I do:

1. Go to the Tasks page (which defaults to showing 'Active tasks' in the filter)
2. Type 'Q2 planning' in the search bar and hit Enter
3. The results show an archived task from last year called 'Q2 Planning Kickoff' but NOT the active task I created yesterday with a similar name

The archived task shows up mixed in with other results — it's not the only result, but it's there when it shouldn't be. And the active one I expect to see is missing.

Actually, what's really strange is that if I manually switch the filter to 'Archived tasks', I can see my *active* tasks showing up there instead. It's like the filter is backwards — 'Active' shows archived tasks and 'Archived' shows active ones. But when I click into any individual task, it correctly shows whether it's archived or active. It's only the search/filter that seems confused.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for that excellent walkthrough — the detail about the filter appearing to work *backwards* is a really valuable clue. That narrows things down a lot.

One more thing that would help us pin this down: could you share your environment details?

- **Browser and version** (e.g., Chrome 124, Firefox 126)
- **Operating system** (e.g., Windows 11, macOS Sonoma)
- **TaskFlow version or plan** (if visible — sometimes shown in Settings or the page footer)

This will help us determine whether this is tied to a specific release or environment. Thanks!

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm on Chrome, latest version — I think 124 or 125, I'm not sure of the exact number. Running Windows 11. As for TaskFlow, we're on v2.3.1 — we actually just had an update about three days ago. Come to think of it, I believe the search issues started right around that update. I didn't connect the two at first, but the timing lines up.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search/filter inverted: 'Active tasks' filter returns archived tasks and vice versa since v2.3.1","problem":"The task list filter is reversed — selecting 'Active tasks' displays archived tasks in search results, and selecting 'Archived tasks' displays active tasks. Individual task detail pages correctly show the archived/active status, so the underlying data is correct but the filter/query logic is inverted.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3.1 update (released ~3 days ago) likely introduced a regression in the search/filter query logic — most probably a boolean inversion or swapped enum value when filtering by task status (e.g., `is_archived = true` where it should be `false`, or active/archived filter IDs were transposed).","reproduction_steps":["Go to the Tasks page (defaults to 'Active tasks' filter)","Type 'Q2 planning' (or any known term) in the main search bar and press Enter","Observe that archived tasks appear in results while known active tasks are missing","Switch the filter to 'Archived tasks'","Observe that active tasks now appear in the archived results"],"environment":"Chrome ~124-125, Windows 11, TaskFlow v2.3.1. Confirmed by at least two users (reporter and teammate).","severity":"high","impact":"All users performing searches or using the task list filter see inverted results. Active tasks are effectively hidden from normal workflow, and archived tasks clutter the default view. This affects core usability for every user on v2.3.1.","recommended_fix":"Inspect the v2.3.1 diff for changes to the search/filter query logic — specifically the status filter predicate. Look for an inverted boolean condition or swapped enum/constant when translating the UI filter selection ('Active' vs 'Archived') into a database or API query parameter. The task detail page correctly shows status, so the data model is fine — the bug is in the list/search query layer.","proposed_test_case":"Create one active task and one archived task with overlapping names. Search with the 'Active tasks' filter and assert only the active task is returned. Switch to 'Archived tasks' filter and assert only the archived task is returned.","information_gaps":["Exact Chrome version (reporter unsure if 124 or 125 — unlikely to matter for a server/logic-side bug)","Whether the issue reproduces on other browsers (given the likely server-side root cause, this is low priority)"]}
